[package]
name = "fathomdb-py"
version.workspace = true
edition.workspace = true
license.workspace = true
repository.workspace = true
rust-version.workspace = true
publish = false

[lib]
name = "fathomdb_py"
crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]

[features]
default = []
test-hooks = []
# EU-6: forward the engine's default-embedder feature so the
# `use_default_embedder=True` kwarg actually materializes a real
# `CandleBgeEmbedder` rather than failing with a typed Embedder error.
# Wheels built without this feature still expose the kwarg but reject
# `True` at open time per ADR-0.7.1-default-embedder-weight-fetch (no
# silent network in a no-feature wheel).
default-embedder = ["fathomdb-engine/default-embedder"]
# 0.8.2 Slice E2 fix-1 [P1]: forward the engine's default-reranker feature so
# a plain `maturin develop` / `pytest` activates the real CE reranker in
# dev/test builds. The shipped wheel keeps `default-reranker` OFF (release.yml
# and ci.yml pin their own explicit `--features` lists that omit it).
default-reranker = ["fathomdb-engine/default-reranker"]
# GPU acceleration (opt-in; forwards to the engine; default wheel stays CPU).
# Build: `maturin develop --features pyo3/extension-module,embed-cuda`; activate via
# FATHOMDB_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda. See dev/design/0.8.1-embedder-gpu-and-portability.md.
embed-cuda = ["default-embedder", "fathomdb-engine/embed-cuda"]
embed-metal = ["default-embedder", "fathomdb-engine/embed-metal"]

[dependencies]
fathomdb-embedder.workspace = true
fathomdb-embedder-api.workspace = true
fathomdb-engine.workspace = true
fathomdb-schema.workspace = true
pyo3 = { version = "0.29", features = ["extension-module", "abi3-py310"] }
serde_json = "1"

1	// pyo3 0.22's `create_exception!` and `#[pymodule]` macros emit
2	// `#[cfg(feature = "gil-refs")]` arms that reference an upstream
3	// feature this crate does not export; the resulting `unexpected_cfgs`
4	// warnings are noise on a clippy `-D warnings` gate. The
5	// `useless_conversion` allow covers `#[pymethods]`-generated PyResult
6	// wrappers that clippy flags as redundant `Into<PyErr>` calls.
7	#![allow(unexpected_cfgs)]
8	#![allow(clippy::useless_conversion)]
9	
10	//! PyO3 binding from the Python SDK to `fathomdb-engine`.
11	//!
12	//! FFI safety contract (mirrored by Phase 11b napi-rs):
13	//!
14	//! 1. Every method that may block inside the engine wraps the call in
15	//!    `py.detach(...)` so the GIL is released for the duration.
16	//! 2. Engine entry points return typed errors via [`engine_error_to_py`] /
17	//!    [`engine_open_error_to_py`] — single-switch mapping with no
18	//!    catch-all arm; the binding fails to compile when the Rust variant
19	//!    set drifts from the Python class set (AC-060a).
20	//! 3. Every string crossing the FFI is checked by [`validate_ffi_string`]
21	//!    for embedded NUL or unpaired UTF-16 surrogates BEFORE the writer
22	//!    transaction opens (AC-068a / AC-068b).
23	//! 4. Panics inside engine code surface as Python `PanicException`
24	//!    instances (PyO3 `pyo3::panic::PanicException`); the host process
25	//!    is not aborted (AC-067). Engine calls are wrapped in
26	//!    `catch_unwind` so the panic is translated on the Rust side rather
27	//!    than relying on PyO3's implicit conversion at the FFI boundary.
28	//!    PanicException is intentionally NOT an `EngineError` subclass:
29	//!    panic is a contract bug, not a typed engine outcome, and callers
30	//!    that catch `EngineError` must not silently swallow it.
31	
32	use std::panic::{catch_unwind, AssertUnwindSafe};
33	use std::sync::Arc;
34	
35	use fathomdb_embedder::EmbedderEvent as RustEmbedderEvent;
36	use fathomdb_embedder_api::EmbedderIdentity as RustEmbedderIdentity;
37	use fathomdb_engine::{
38	    rerank_passages as rust_rerank_passages, ComparisonOp as RustComparisonOp, CorruptionDetail,
39	    CorruptionKind, EmbedderChoice, Engine as RustEngine, EngineError as RustEngineError,
40	    EngineOpenError, ExtractDocument as RustExtractDocument,
41	    IngestWithExtractorReceipt as RustIngestWithExtractorReceipt, NodeRecord as RustNodeRecord,
42	    OpStoreRow as RustOpStoreRow, OpenReport as RustOpenReport, OpenStage,
43	    Predicate as RustPredicate, PreparedWrite, ScalarValue as RustScalarValue,
44	    SearchExpandResult as RustSearchExpandResult, SearchFilter as RustSearchFilter,
45	    SearchHit as RustSearchHit, SearchResult as RustSearchResult, SoftFallback as RustSoftFallback,
46	    SoftFallbackBranch, TraversalDirection as RustTraversalDirection,
47	    WriteReceipt as RustWriteReceipt,
48	};
49	use fathomdb_schema::MigrationStepReport as RustMigrationStepReport;
50	use pyo3::create_exception;
51	use pyo3::exceptions::{PyException, PyTypeError, PyValueError};
52	use pyo3::panic::PanicException;
53	use pyo3::prelude::*;
54	use pyo3::types::{PyDict, PyList};
55	
56	// ===== Exceptions =====================================================
57	//
58	// Root + concrete leaves per dev/design/errors.md § Binding-facing class
59	// matrix. All concrete leaves inherit from `EngineError`; `EngineError`
60	// inherits from Python `Exception` via `create_exception!`.
61	
62	create_exception!(_fathomdb, EngineError, PyException);
63	create_exception!(_fathomdb, StorageError, EngineError);
64	create_exception!(_fathomdb, ProjectionError, EngineError);
65	create_exception!(_fathomdb, VectorError, EngineError);
66	create_exception!(_fathomdb, KindNotVectorIndexedError, VectorError);
67	create_exception!(_fathomdb, EmbedderError, EngineError);
68	create_exception!(_fathomdb, EmbedderNotConfiguredError, EmbedderError);
69	create_exception!(_fathomdb, SchedulerError, EngineError);
70	create_exception!(_fathomdb, OpStoreError, EngineError);
71	create_exception!(_fathomdb, WriteValidationError, EngineError);
72	create_exception!(_fathomdb, SchemaValidationError, EngineError);
73	create_exception!(_fathomdb, OverloadedError, EngineError);
74	create_exception!(_fathomdb, ClosingError, EngineError);
75	create_exception!(_fathomdb, DatabaseLockedError, EngineError);
76	create_exception!(_fathomdb, CorruptionError, EngineError);
77	create_exception!(_fathomdb, IncompatibleSchemaVersionError, EngineError);
78	create_exception!(_fathomdb, MigrationError, EngineError);
79	create_exception!(_fathomdb, EmbedderIdentityMismatchError, EngineError);
80	create_exception!(_fathomdb, EmbedderDimensionMismatchError, EngineError);
81	// G11 (Slice 15) — BYO-LLM extraction harness protocol error.
82	create_exception!(_fathomdb, ExtractorError, EngineError);
83	// G4 (Slice 35) — filter predicate construction error (non-allowlisted path).
84	create_exception!(_fathomdb, InvalidFilterError, EngineError);
85	// Slice 20 (G5/G6) — traversal depth > 3 or other out-of-range argument.
86	create_exception!(_fathomdb, InvalidArgumentError, EngineError);
87	
88	// ===== String validation (AC-068a / AC-068b) =========================
89	
90	/// Reject strings carrying an embedded NUL or an unpaired UTF-16
91	/// surrogate codepoint (`U+D800..=U+DFFF`).
92	///
93	/// Both are valid Python `str` values but invalid for SQLite text
94	/// columns; AC-068a/b requires the binding to reject them BEFORE the
95	/// writer transaction opens (no-row-written invariant).
96	pub fn validate_ffi_string(value: &str) -> Result<(), String> {
97	    if value.as_bytes().contains(&0) {
98	        return Err("embedded NUL byte in FFI string".to_string());
99	    }
100	    for ch in value.chars() {
101	        let cp = ch as u32;
102	        if (0xD800..=0xDFFF).contains(&cp) {
103	            return Err(format!("unpaired UTF-16 surrogate U+{cp:04X} in FFI string"));
104	        }
105	    }
106	    Ok(())
107	}
108	
109	fn validate_ffi_string_py(value: &str) -> PyResult<()> {
110	    validate_ffi_string(value).map_err(WriteValidationError::new_err)
111	}
112	
113	/// Extract a Python string into a Rust `String` and run
114	/// [`validate_ffi_string_py`]. PyO3's built-in `str` extraction already
115	/// fails on lone surrogates (the underlying `PyUnicode_AsUTF8AndSize`
116	/// raises `UnicodeEncodeError`); we re-raise those as the typed
117	/// `WriteValidationError` so callers can dispatch on a single class.
118	fn extract_validated_str(value: &Bound<'_, PyAny>) -> PyResult<String> {
119	    match value.extract::<String>() {
120	        Ok(s) => {
121	            validate_ffi_string_py(&s)?;
122	            Ok(s)
123	        }
124	        Err(_) => Err(WriteValidationError::new_err(
125	            "string contains characters not representable as UTF-8 (lone surrogate)",
126	        )),
127	    }
128	}
129	
130	/// `Option` lift of [`extract_validated_str`]: `None`/`None`-valued stays
131	/// `None` (preserving the all-`None` byte-identical unfiltered path); a
132	/// present value is extracted and validated through the same FFI gate as the
133	/// write path. Used by `search` for the G10 `SearchFilter` string fields.
134	fn extract_opt_validated_str(value: Option<&Bound<'_, PyAny>>) -> PyResult<Option<String>> {
135	    match value {
136	        Some(v) if !v.is_none() => Ok(Some(extract_validated_str(v)?)),
137	        _ => Ok(None),
138	    }
139	}
140	
141	// ===== Error mapping ==================================================
142	
143	/// Translate every `EngineError` variant to its Python counterpart.
144	///
145	/// No catch-all arm: drift between the Rust enum and the Python class
146	/// set is a compile error.
147	fn engine_error_to_py(err: RustEngineError) -> PyErr {
148	    match err {
149	        RustEngineError::Storage => StorageError::new_err("storage error"),
150	        RustEngineError::Projection => ProjectionError::new_err("projection error"),
151	        RustEngineError::Vector => VectorError::new_err("vector error"),
152	        RustEngineError::Embedder => EmbedderError::new_err("embedder error"),
153	        RustEngineError::EmbedderNotConfigured => {
154	            EmbedderNotConfiguredError::new_err("embedder is not configured")
155	        }
156	        RustEngineError::KindNotVectorIndexed => {
157	            KindNotVectorIndexedError::new_err("kind is not configured for vector indexing")
158	        }
159	        RustEngineError::EmbedderDimensionMismatch { expected, actual } => {
160	            let exc = EmbedderDimensionMismatchError::new_err(format!(
161	                "embedder vector dimension mismatch: stored {expected}, supplied {actual}",
162	            ));
163	            Python::attach(|py| {
164	                let v = exc.value(py);
165	                let _ = v.setattr("stored", expected);
166	                let _ = v.setattr("supplied", actual);
167	            });
168	            exc
169	        }
170	        RustEngineError::Scheduler => SchedulerError::new_err("scheduler error"),
171	        RustEngineError::OpStore => OpStoreError::new_err("op-store error"),
172	        RustEngineError::WriteValidation => WriteValidationError::new_err("write validation error"),
173	        RustEngineError::SchemaValidation => {
174	            SchemaValidationError::new_err("schema validation error")
175	        }
176	        RustEngineError::Overloaded => OverloadedError::new_err("engine overloaded"),
177	        RustEngineError::Closing => ClosingError::new_err("engine is closing"),
178	        RustEngineError::Extractor => ExtractorError::new_err("extractor error"),
179	        RustEngineError::InvalidFilter { reason } => {
180	            InvalidFilterError::new_err(format!("invalid filter: {reason}"))
181	        }
182	        RustEngineError::InvalidArgument { msg } => InvalidArgumentError::new_err(msg),
183	    }
184	}
185	
186	fn corruption_kind_str(kind: CorruptionKind) -> &'static str {
187	    match kind {
188	        CorruptionKind::WalReplayFailure => "WalReplayFailure",
189	        CorruptionKind::HeaderMalformed => "HeaderMalformed",
190	        CorruptionKind::SchemaInconsistent => "SchemaInconsistent",
191	        CorruptionKind::EmbedderIdentityDrift => "EmbedderIdentityDrift",
192	    }
193	}
194	
195	fn open_stage_str(stage: OpenStage) -> &'static str {
196	    match stage {
197	        OpenStage::HeaderProbe => "HeaderProbe",
198	        OpenStage::WalReplay => "WalReplay",
199	        OpenStage::SchemaProbe => "SchemaProbe",
200	        OpenStage::EmbedderIdentity => "EmbedderIdentity",
201	    }
202	}
203	
204	fn engine_open_error_to_py(err: EngineOpenError) -> PyErr {
205	    match err {
206	        EngineOpenError::DatabaseLocked { holder_pid } => {
207	            let exc = DatabaseLockedError::new_err(match holder_pid {
208	                Some(pid) => format!("database is locked by process {pid}"),
209	                None => "database is locked by another engine instance".to_string(),
210	            });
211	            Python::attach(|py| {
212	                let _ = exc.value(py).setattr("holder_pid", holder_pid);
213	            });
214	            exc
215	        }
216	        EngineOpenError::Corruption(detail) => corruption_to_py(detail),
217	        EngineOpenError::IncompatibleSchemaVersion { seen, supported } => {
218	            IncompatibleSchemaVersionError::new_err(format!(
219	                "database schema version {seen} is incompatible with supported version {supported}"
220	            ))
221	        }
222	        EngineOpenError::MigrationError {
223	            schema_version_before,
224	            schema_version_current,
225	            step_id,
226	        } => MigrationError::new_err(format!(
227	            "schema migration failed at step {step_id}; schema version remained between {schema_version_before} and {schema_version_current}"
228	        )),
229	        EngineOpenError::EmbedderIdentityMismatch { stored, supplied } => {
230	            let exc = EmbedderIdentityMismatchError::new_err(format!(
231	                "embedder identity mismatch: stored {}@{}, supplied {}@{}",
232	                stored.name, stored.revision, supplied.name, supplied.revision,
233	            ));
234	            Python::attach(|py| {
235	                let v = exc.value(py);
236	                let _ = v.setattr("stored_name", stored.name);
237	                let _ = v.setattr("stored_revision", stored.revision);
238	                let _ = v.setattr("supplied_name", supplied.name);
239	                let _ = v.setattr("supplied_revision", supplied.revision);
240	            });
241	            exc
242	        }
243	        EngineOpenError::EmbedderDimensionMismatch { stored, supplied } => {
244	            let exc = EmbedderDimensionMismatchError::new_err(format!(
245	                "embedder vector dimension mismatch: stored {stored}, supplied {supplied}",
246	            ));
247	            Python::attach(|py| {
248	                let v = exc.value(py);
249	                let _ = v.setattr("stored", stored);
250	                let _ = v.setattr("supplied", supplied);
251	            });
252	            exc
253	        }
254	        EngineOpenError::Embedder(err) => EmbedderError::new_err(format!("{err:?}")),
255	        EngineOpenError::Io { message } => {
256	            StorageError::new_err(format!("database I/O error: {message}"))
257	        }
258	    }
259	}
260	
261	fn corruption_to_py(detail: CorruptionDetail) -> PyErr {
262	    let kind = corruption_kind_str(detail.kind);
263	    let stage = open_stage_str(detail.stage);
264	    let recovery_hint_code = detail.recovery_hint.code;
265	    let doc_anchor = detail.recovery_hint.doc_anchor;
266	    let exc = CorruptionError::new_err(format!(
267	        "corruption {kind} at stage {stage} ({recovery_hint_code})"
268	    ));
269	    Python::attach(|py| {
270	        let v = exc.value(py);
271	        let _ = v.setattr("kind", kind);
272	        let _ = v.setattr("stage", stage);
273	        let _ = v.setattr("recovery_hint_code", recovery_hint_code);
274	        let _ = v.setattr("doc_anchor", doc_anchor);
275	    });
276	    exc
277	}
278	
279	/// Run the engine call inside `py.detach` and `catch_unwind`;
280	/// translate any escaping panic to `EngineError`.
281	///
282	/// `AssertUnwindSafe` wraps the caller's closure so we do not need to
283	/// require `UnwindSafe` from `f`. The engine's `Arc<dyn Embedder>`
284	/// makes the natural `UnwindSafe` bound unsatisfiable; the engine
285	/// itself takes care of its own atomicity post-panic.
286	fn call_engine<R: Send>(
287	    py: Python<'_>,
288	    f: impl FnOnce() -> Result<R, RustEngineError> + Send,
289	) -> PyResult<R> {
290	    let wrapped = AssertUnwindSafe(f);
291	    let result = py.detach(|| catch_unwind(wrapped));
292	    match result {
293	        Ok(Ok(value)) => Ok(value),
294	        Ok(Err(err)) => Err(engine_error_to_py(err)),
295	        Err(_) => Err(PanicException::new_err("engine panic (see logs)")),
296	    }
297	}
298	
299	// ===== Data classes ===================================================
300	
301	#[pyclass(
302	    module = "fathomdb._fathomdb",
303	    name = "WriteReceipt",
304	    frozen,
305	    get_all,
306	    skip_from_py_object
307	)]
308	#[derive(Clone)]
309	struct PyWriteReceipt {
310	    cursor: u64,
311	    /// G0 (Slice 15) — per-row `write_cursor`s, 1:1 with the input batch order.

589	/// Serialise one [`RustEmbedderEvent`] as a Python `dict`. The `kind`
590	/// key carries the variant name (`"DefaultEmbedderDownload"`,
591	/// `"DefaultEmbedderCacheHit"`, `"MeanVecPinned"`); the remaining keys
592	/// carry the variant payload in snake_case. We pick a dict (rather than
593	/// a per-variant `#[pyclass]`) so callers can pattern-match on the
594	/// `"kind"` discriminant without importing leaf classes.
595	fn embedder_event_to_py(py: Python<'_>, ev: &RustEmbedderEvent) -> Py<PyAny> {
596	    let dict = PyDict::new(py);
597	    match ev {
598	        RustEmbedderEvent::DefaultEmbedderDownload {
599	            file,
600	            url,
601	            bytes,
602	            sha256,
603	            cache_path,
604	            duration_ms,
605	        } => {
606	            let _ = dict.set_item("kind", "DefaultEmbedderDownload");
607	            let _ = dict.set_item("file", file);
608	            let _ = dict.set_item("url", url);
609	            let _ = dict.set_item("bytes", *bytes);
610	            let _ = dict.set_item("sha256", sha256);
611	            let _ = dict.set_item("cache_path", cache_path.display().to_string());
612	            let _ = dict.set_item("duration_ms", *duration_ms);
613	        }
614	        RustEmbedderEvent::DefaultEmbedderCacheHit { file, sha256, cache_path } => {
615	            let _ = dict.set_item("kind", "DefaultEmbedderCacheHit");
616	            let _ = dict.set_item("file", file);
617	            let _ = dict.set_item("sha256", sha256);
618	            let _ = dict.set_item("cache_path", cache_path.display().to_string());
619	        }
620	        RustEmbedderEvent::MeanVecPinned { dim, doc_count } => {
621	            let _ = dict.set_item("kind", "MeanVecPinned");
622	            let _ = dict.set_item("dim", *dim);
623	            let _ = dict.set_item("doc_count", *doc_count);
624	        }
625	        RustEmbedderEvent::MeanVecRecomputed { dim, doc_count, trigger } => {
626	            let _ = dict.set_item("kind", "MeanVecRecomputed");
627	            let _ = dict.set_item("dim", *dim);
628	            let _ = dict.set_item("doc_count", *doc_count);
629	            let _ = dict.set_item("trigger", trigger.as_str());
630	        }
631	    }
632	    dict.into()
633	}

678	        let prepared = translate_batch(&batch)?;

684	    /// G10 + 0.8.1 R1 — hybrid search with an optional closed metadata filter
685	    /// and an optional CE rerank depth. Each filter field is an optional kwarg;
686	    /// all-`None` is the unfiltered (byte-identical) path. `rerank_depth=0`
687	    /// (default) keeps the identity / soft-fallback path. `rerank_depth > 0`
688	    /// activates CE reranking over the top-N fused hits (when the
689	    /// `default-reranker` feature is enabled and the model is loaded; otherwise
690	    /// falls back to identity).
691	    // 0.8.1 R1/R3: rerank_depth and use_graph_arm add 8th arg; suppress lint.
692	    #[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
693	    #[pyo3(
694	        signature = (query, source_type=None, kind=None, created_after=None,
695	                     status=None, rerank_depth=0, use_graph_arm=false,
696	                     alpha=None, pool_n=None)
697	    )]
698	    fn search(
699	        &self,
700	        py: Python<'_>,
701	        query: &str,
702	        source_type: Option<Bound<'_, PyAny>>,
703	        kind: Option<Bound<'_, PyAny>>,
704	        created_after: Option<i64>,
705	        status: Option<Bound<'_, PyAny>>,
706	        rerank_depth: usize,
707	        // 0.8.1 R3 (Slice 30) — when True, seed BFS over temporal fact-edges
708	        // from the top-10 fused hits and fuse reachable nodes as a third RRF arm.
709	        // Default False → byte-identical to the pre-Slice-30 two-arm pipeline.
710	        use_graph_arm: bool,
711	        // 0.8.5 (EXP-0) — CE-rerank knobs. `alpha` (default 0.3) is the CE-blend
712	        // weight, clamped to [0,1] in the engine; `pool_n` (default = rerank_depth)
713	        // is the reranked-pool size. Omitting both reproduces the byte-identical
714	        // default ranking; `alpha=1.0, pool_n=10` is the measured-parity config.
715	        alpha: Option<f64>,
716	        pool_n: Option<usize>,
717	    ) -> PyResult<PySearchResult> {
718	        validate_ffi_string_py(query)?;
719	        // G10 filter strings cross the FFI exactly like `query` and the write
720	        // fields, so they go through the same validation gate
721	        // (`extract_validated_str`: rejects embedded NUL and lone UTF-16
722	        // surrogate as the typed `WriteValidationError`). `None` stays `None`
723	        // so the all-`None` filter remains the byte-identical unfiltered path.
724	        let source_type = extract_opt_validated_str(source_type.as_ref())?;
725	        let kind = extract_opt_validated_str(kind.as_ref())?;
726	        let status = extract_opt_validated_str(status.as_ref())?;
727	        let engine = Arc::clone(&self.inner);
728	        let query = query.to_string();
729	        let filter = if source_type.is_some()
730	            || kind.is_some()
731	            || created_after.is_some()
732	            || status.is_some()
733	        {
734	            Some(RustSearchFilter { source_type, kind, created_after, status })
735	        } else {
736	            None
737	        };
738	        // 0.8.1 R1: use search_reranked so rerank_depth=0 is a no-op (identity)
739	        // and rerank_depth>0 activates the CE path.
740	        // 0.8.1 R3: use_graph_arm=True activates the graph-BFS third arm.
741	        // 0.8.5 (D4): resolve the binding-side defaults — α=0.3, pool_n=rerank_depth —
742	        // so an unset call reproduces the pre-slice ranking exactly. α is clamped in
743	        // the engine's `ce_rerank`.
744	        let alpha = alpha.unwrap_or(0.3);
745	        let pool_n = pool_n.unwrap_or(rerank_depth);
746	        let result = call_engine(py, move || {
747	            engine.search_reranked(&query, filter, rerank_depth, use_graph_arm, alpha, pool_n)
748	        })?;
749	        Ok(PySearchResult::from_rust(result))
750	    }

980	// ===== read.list (G4 / Slice 35) ======================================
981	//
982	// `read.list(engine, kind, predicates?, limit)` — list active canonical nodes
983	// of a given `kind`, optionally filtered by a list of `Predicate` dicts.
984	// Each predicate dict has the shape:
985	//   { "type": "eq"|"gt"|"gte"|"lt"|"lte", "path": str, "value": str|int|bool }
986	// Path validation happens in Rust (InvalidFilterError on non-allowlisted path).
987	
988	fn py_predicate_to_rust(pred: &Bound<'_, PyAny>) -> PyResult<RustPredicate> {
989	    let type_item = pred.get_item("type")?;
990	    let type_str = extract_validated_str(&type_item)?;
991	    let path_item = pred.get_item("path")?;
992	    let path = extract_validated_str(&path_item)?;
993	    let value_obj = pred.get_item("value")?;
994	
995	    // Extract the value — try bool first (Python bool is a subclass of int, so
996	    // bool must be checked before int to avoid misclassifying True/False).
997	    // String values are validated through extract_validated_str for FFI safety.
998	    let scalar: RustScalarValue = if let Ok(b) = value_obj.extract::<bool>() {
999	        RustScalarValue::Bool(b)
1000	    } else if let Ok(i) = value_obj.extract::<i64>() {
1001	        RustScalarValue::Integer(i)
1002	    } else {
1003	        RustScalarValue::Text(extract_validated_str(&value_obj)?)
1004	    };
1005	
1006	    match type_str.as_str() {
1007	        "eq" => RustPredicate::json_path_eq(path, scalar).map_err(engine_error_to_py),
1008	        "gt" => RustPredicate::json_path_compare(path, RustComparisonOp::Gt, scalar)
1009	            .map_err(engine_error_to_py),
1010	        "gte" => RustPredicate::json_path_compare(path, RustComparisonOp::Gte, scalar)
1011	            .map_err(engine_error_to_py),
1012	        "lt" => RustPredicate::json_path_compare(path, RustComparisonOp::Lt, scalar)
1013	            .map_err(engine_error_to_py),
1014	        "lte" => RustPredicate::json_path_compare(path, RustComparisonOp::Lte, scalar)
1015	            .map_err(engine_error_to_py),
1016	        other => Err(pyo3::exceptions::PyValueError::new_err(format!(
1017	            "unknown predicate type '{other}'; expected 'eq', 'gt', 'gte', 'lt', or 'lte'"
1018	        ))),
1019	    }
1020	}

# Distilled technical summary — fathomdb-py binding + STATUS-0.8.9

> Written from held memory (the raw `Cargo.toml`, `src/lib.rs`, and `STATUS-0.8.9.md`
> were read in full earlier this session and are NOT re-read for this distillation).
> Dense on names/values, terse on prose. Scope: the PyO3 binding crate
> `fathomdb-py` (the Python SDK FFI to `fathomdb-engine`) plus the 0.8.9 CI-integrity
> status board.

---

## 1. Crate manifest — `src/rust/crates/fathomdb-py/Cargo.toml`

### `[package]`
- `name = "fathomdb-py"`
- `version.workspace = true`
- `edition.workspace = true`
- `license.workspace = true`
- `repository.workspace = true`
- `rust-version.workspace = true`
- `publish = false` (never published to crates.io; the wheel is the deliverable)

### `[lib]`
- `name = "fathomdb_py"`
- `crate-type = ["cdylib", "rlib"]` — cdylib for the Python extension module, rlib so
  the crate can be unit-tested / linked from Rust.

### `[features]` — SIX flags (NOT five)
1. `default = []` — empty default set; release wheels build with explicit `--features`
   lists (CI/release pin their own), so `default` carries nothing.
2. `test-hooks = []` — gates the test-only FFI seams; **this is the single feature
   referenced via a `cfg` attribute inside `lib.rs`** (`#[cfg(any(test, feature =
   "test-hooks"))]`). The other four features only forward to the engine in
   Cargo.toml and are never named in a `lib.rs` cfg.
3. `default-embedder = ["fathomdb-engine/default-embedder"]` — EU-6: forwards the
   engine's default-embedder feature so `use_default_embedder=True` materializes a real
   `CandleBgeEmbedder` instead of failing with a typed Embedder error. No-feature wheels
   still expose the kwarg but reject `True` at open time (no silent network) per
   ADR-0.7.1-default-embedder-weight-fetch.
4. `default-reranker = ["fathomdb-engine/default-reranker"]` — 0.8.2 Slice E2 fix-1
   [P1]: forwards the engine's default-reranker so a plain `maturin develop` / `pytest`
   activates the real CE reranker in dev/test. Shipped wheel keeps `default-reranker`
   OFF (release.yml + ci.yml pin explicit `--features` lists omitting it).
5. `embed-cuda = ["default-embedder", "fathomdb-engine/embed-cuda"]` — opt-in GPU
   (CUDA); forwards to engine; default wheel stays CPU. Build:
   `maturin develop --features pyo3/extension-module,embed-cuda`; activate via
   `FATHOMDB_EMBED_DEVICE=cuda`. See dev/design/0.8.1-embedder-gpu-and-portability.md.
6. `embed-metal = ["default-embedder", "fathomdb-engine/embed-metal"]` — opt-in GPU
   (Apple Metal); same forwarding pattern.

### `[dependencies]`
- `fathomdb-embedder.workspace = true`
- `fathomdb-embedder-api.workspace = true`
- `fathomdb-engine.workspace = true`
- `fathomdb-schema.workspace = true`
- `pyo3 = { version = "0.29", features = ["extension-module", "abi3-py310"] }`
  — **pyo3 version pin = "0.29"**, features `extension-module` + `abi3-py310`
  (stable ABI targeting Python 3.10+).
- `serde_json = "1"`

---

## 2. `src/lib.rs` — overview (1489 lines, held in full)

PyO3 binding from the Python SDK to `fathomdb-engine`. Crate-level attributes:
- `#![allow(unexpected_cfgs)]` — pyo3's `create_exception!`/`#[pymodule]` macros emit
  `#[cfg(feature = "gil-refs")]` arms referencing a feature this crate doesn't export;
  the resulting `unexpected_cfgs` warnings are noise on a clippy `-D warnings` gate.
- `#![allow(clippy::useless_conversion)]` — covers `#[pymethods]`-generated PyResult
  wrappers flagged as redundant `Into<PyErr>` calls.

### FFI safety contract (module doc; mirrored by Phase 11b napi-rs)
1. Every potentially-blocking engine call wraps in `py.detach(...)` to release the GIL.
2. Engine entry points return typed errors via `engine_error_to_py` /
   `engine_open_error_to_py` — single-switch mapping, **no catch-all arm**; the binding
   fails to compile when the Rust variant set drifts from the Python class set (AC-060a).
3. Every string crossing the FFI is checked by `validate_ffi_string` for embedded NUL or
   unpaired UTF-16 surrogates BEFORE the writer transaction opens (AC-068a / AC-068b).
4. Panics inside engine code surface as Python `PanicException`
   (`pyo3::panic::PanicException`); host process is not aborted (AC-067). Engine calls are
   `catch_unwind`-wrapped so the panic is translated Rust-side. PanicException is
   intentionally NOT an `EngineError` subclass: panic = contract bug, not a typed outcome.

## 3. Exception hierarchy (the `create_exception!` matrix)

Module name in `create_exception!` = `_fathomdb`. Root inherits from Python `Exception`
via `PyException`. Per dev/design/errors.md § Binding-facing class matrix.

### Root
- **`EngineError`** ← `PyException` (the ROOT typed exception; all concrete leaves
  inherit from it).

### Direct children of `EngineError` (one level deep)
- `StorageError`
- `ProjectionError`
- `VectorError`
- `EmbedderError`
- `SchedulerError`
- `OpStoreError`
- `WriteValidationError`
- `SchemaValidationError`
- `OverloadedError`
- `ClosingError`
- `DatabaseLockedError`
- `CorruptionError`
- `IncompatibleSchemaVersionError`
- `MigrationError`
- `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError`
- `EmbedderDimensionMismatchError`
- `ExtractorError` — G11 (Slice 15) BYO-LLM extraction harness protocol error.
- `InvalidFilterError` — G4 (Slice 35) filter predicate construction error
  (non-allowlisted path).
- `InvalidArgumentError` — Slice 20 (G5/G6) traversal depth > 3 / out-of-range arg.

### Two-level-deep leaves (nested under a non-root leaf)
- **`KindNotVectorIndexedError`** ← `VectorError` ← `EngineError`
- **`EmbedderNotConfiguredError`** ← `EmbedderError` ← `EngineError`

### NOT in the hierarchy
- `PanicException` (pyo3 built-in) — deliberately NOT an `EngineError` subclass.

### Module registration (`m.add(...)`) — every exception added by name
`EngineError`, `StorageError`, `ProjectionError`, `VectorError`,
`KindNotVectorIndexedError`, `EmbedderError`, `EmbedderNotConfiguredError`,
`SchedulerError`, `OpStoreError`, `WriteValidationError`, `SchemaValidationError`,
`OverloadedError`, `ClosingError`, `DatabaseLockedError`, `CorruptionError`,
`IncompatibleSchemaVersionError`, `MigrationError`, `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError`,
`EmbedderDimensionMismatchError`, `ExtractorError`, `InvalidFilterError`,
`InvalidArgumentError`.

## 4. String validation (AC-068a / AC-068b)

- `pub fn validate_ffi_string(value: &str) -> Result<(), String>` — rejects embedded NUL
  byte ("embedded NUL byte in FFI string") and any codepoint in `0xD800..=0xDFFF`
  ("unpaired UTF-16 surrogate U+{cp:04X} in FFI string"). Both are valid Python `str` but
  invalid for SQLite text columns; rejected BEFORE the writer tx opens (no-row-written).
- `fn validate_ffi_string_py(value: &str) -> PyResult<()>` — maps the error to
  `WriteValidationError`.
- `fn extract_validated_str(value: &Bound<'_, PyAny>) -> PyResult<String>` — extract
  Python str → Rust String + validate; lone-surrogate extraction failure re-raised as
  `WriteValidationError` ("string contains characters not representable as UTF-8 (lone
  surrogate)").
- `fn extract_opt_validated_str(value: Option<&Bound<'_, PyAny>>) -> PyResult<Option<String>>`
  — Option lift: `None`/None-valued stays `None` (preserves all-`None` byte-identical
  unfiltered path); present value validated through the same gate. Used by `search` for
  the G10 `SearchFilter` string fields.

## 5. Error mapping functions (no catch-all)

### `fn engine_error_to_py(err: RustEngineError) -> PyErr`
Exhaustive match — drift = compile error. Arms:
- `Storage` → `StorageError("storage error")`
- `Projection` → `ProjectionError("projection error")`
- `Vector` → `VectorError("vector error")`
- `Embedder` → `EmbedderError("embedder error")`
- `EmbedderNotConfigured` → `EmbedderNotConfiguredError("embedder is not configured")`
- `KindNotVectorIndexed` → `KindNotVectorIndexedError("kind is not configured for vector indexing")`
- `EmbedderDimensionMismatch { expected, actual }` → `EmbedderDimensionMismatchError`,
  sets attrs `stored=expected`, `supplied=actual`.
- `Scheduler` → `SchedulerError`
- `OpStore` → `OpStoreError`
- `WriteValidation` → `WriteValidationError`
- `SchemaValidation` → `SchemaValidationError`
- `Overloaded` → `OverloadedError("engine overloaded")`
- `Closing` → `ClosingError("engine is closing")`
- `Extractor` → `ExtractorError("extractor error")`
- `InvalidFilter { reason }` → `InvalidFilterError("invalid filter: {reason}")`
- `InvalidArgument { msg }` → `InvalidArgumentError(msg)`

### `fn corruption_kind_str(kind: CorruptionKind) -> &'static str`
- `WalReplayFailure` → "WalReplayFailure"
- `HeaderMalformed` → "HeaderMalformed"
- `SchemaInconsistent` → "SchemaInconsistent"
- `EmbedderIdentityDrift` → "EmbedderIdentityDrift"

### `fn open_stage_str(stage: OpenStage) -> &'static str`
- `HeaderProbe` → "HeaderProbe"
- `WalReplay` → "WalReplay"
- `SchemaProbe` → "SchemaProbe"
- `EmbedderIdentity` → "EmbedderIdentity"

### `fn engine_open_error_to_py(err: EngineOpenError) -> PyErr`
- `DatabaseLocked { holder_pid }` → `DatabaseLockedError`; message varies on
  `Some(pid)`/`None`; sets attr `holder_pid`.
- `Corruption(detail)` → `corruption_to_py(detail)`
- `IncompatibleSchemaVersion { seen, supported }` → `IncompatibleSchemaVersionError`
- `MigrationError { schema_version_before, schema_version_current, step_id }` →
  `MigrationError`
- `EmbedderIdentityMismatch { stored, supplied }` → `EmbedderIdentityMismatchError`; sets
  attrs `stored_name`, `stored_revision`, `supplied_name`, `supplied_revision`.
- `EmbedderDimensionMismatch { stored, supplied }` → `EmbedderDimensionMismatchError`;
  sets attrs `stored`, `supplied`.
- `Embedder(err)` → `EmbedderError("{err:?}")`
- `Io { message }` → `StorageError("database I/O error: {message}")`

### `fn corruption_to_py(detail: CorruptionDetail) -> PyErr`
→ `CorruptionError("corruption {kind} at stage {stage} ({recovery_hint_code})")`; sets
attrs `kind`, `stage`, `recovery_hint_code`, `doc_anchor`.

### `fn call_engine<R: Send>(py, f: impl FnOnce() -> Result<R, RustEngineError> + Send) -> PyResult<R>`
Wraps `f` in `AssertUnwindSafe`, runs inside `py.detach(|| catch_unwind(wrapped))`.
- `Ok(Ok(value))` → `Ok(value)`
- `Ok(Err(err))` → `Err(engine_error_to_py(err))`
- `Err(_)` → `Err(PanicException::new_err("engine panic (see logs)"))`
`AssertUnwindSafe` used because the engine's `Arc<dyn Embedder>` makes the natural
`UnwindSafe` bound unsatisfiable.

Most are `frozen, get_all, skip_from_py_object` + `#[derive(Clone)]`, with a
`from_rust` constructor.

1. **`PyWriteReceipt`** (name `WriteReceipt`) — fields: `cursor: u64`,
   `row_cursors: Vec<u64>` (G0 Slice 15, 1:1 with input batch order),
   `dangling_edge_endpoints: u64` (G8 Slice 20, flag-and-count). `from_rust(RustWriteReceipt)`.

### `embedder_event_to_py(py, ev: &RustEmbedderEvent) -> Py<PyAny>`
Serializes one event as a dict keyed by `"kind"`:

- `MeanVecPinned { dim, doc_count }` → kind "MeanVecPinned".
- `MeanVecRecomputed { dim, doc_count, trigger }` → kind "MeanVecRecomputed"

## 7. `PyEngine` methods (`#[pymethods] impl PyEngine`)

1. **`open`** — `#[staticmethod]`, `#[pyo3(signature = (path, use_default_embedder = false))]`.
   Validates path; `py.detach` + `catch_unwind` around `RustEngine::open_with_choice(path,
   choice)`; choice = `EmbedderChoice::Default` if `use_default_embedder` else
   `EmbedderChoice::None` (EU-6; caller-supplied custom embedders deferred per
   ADR-0.6.0-embedder-protocol Invariant 3). Panic → `PanicException("engine panic during
   open")`; open error → `engine_open_error_to_py`. Returns `Self { inner, open_report }`.
2. **`open_report`** — `(&self, py) -> PyOpenReport` via `PyOpenReport::from_rust`.
3. **`write`** — `(&self, py, batch: Bound<PyList>) -> PyResult<PyWriteReceipt>`.
   `translate_batch` → `engine.write(&prepared)` via `call_engine`.
4. **`search`** — `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`,
   signature `(query, source_type=None, kind=None, created_after=None, status=None,
   rerank_depth=0, use_graph_arm=false, alpha=None, pool_n=None)`. G10 + 0.8.1 R1/R3 +
   0.8.5 EXP-0. Validates query + filter strings via `extract_opt_validated_str`. Builds
   `RustSearchFilter { source_type, kind, created_after, status }` only if any present
   (else `None` = byte-identical unfiltered). Binding-side defaults: `alpha =
   alpha.unwrap_or(0.3)`, `pool_n = pool_n.unwrap_or(rerank_depth)`. Calls
   `engine.search_reranked(&query, filter, rerank_depth, use_graph_arm, alpha, pool_n)`.
   `rerank_depth=0` = identity/soft-fallback; `>0` = CE rerank. `use_graph_arm=true` =
   BFS third RRF arm seeded from top-10 fused hits over temporal fact-edges.
   `alpha=1.0, pool_n=10` = measured-parity config.
5. **`close`** — `(&self, py) -> PyResult<()>` → `engine.close()`.
6. **`drain`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (timeout_s = 0.0))]`; ms =
   `(timeout_s*1000) as u64` if finite & >0 else 0; → `engine.drain(ms)`.
7. **`ingest_with_extractor`** — `(&self, py, cmd: Bound<PyList>, documents: Bound<PyList>)
   -> PyResult<PyIngestWithExtractorReceipt>` (G11 Slice 15). cmd → `Vec<String>` (argv;
   non-string → `WriteValidationError("cmd elements must be strings")`); documents → dicts
   with required `source_doc_id` + `body` → `Vec<RustExtractDocument>`; →
   `engine.ingest_with_extractor(&cmd_refs, &docs)`.
8. **`counters`** — `(&self) -> PyCounterSnapshot` from `self.inner.counters()`.
9. **`set_profiling`** — `(&self, enabled: bool) -> PyResult<()>` →
   `self.inner.set_profiling(enabled)` mapped via `engine_error_to_py`.
10. **`set_slow_threshold_ms`** — `(&self, value: u64) -> PyResult<()>` →
    `self.inner.set_slow_threshold_ms(value)`.
11. **`embed`** — `(&self, py, text: &str) -> PyResult<Vec<f32>>`. Embeds with the pinned
    default embedder `fathomdb-bge-small-en-v1.5`; raises `EmbedderNotConfiguredError` if
    opened without an embedder. Validates text; → `engine.embed_text(&text)`.
12. **`_configure_vector_kind_for_test`** — `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]`.
    EU-6 test-only vector write seam; → `engine.configure_vector_kind_for_test(&kind)`.
13. **`_write_vector_for_test`** — `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]`; →
    `engine.write_vector_for_test(&kind, &text)`.
14. **`attach_logging_subscriber`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (logger,
    heartbeat_interval_ms = None))]`. No-op stub today (subscriber wiring lands in a
    later 0.6.x slice); accepts the call so callers can wire a logger.

## 8. Standalone `#[pyfunction]`s

1. **`admin_configure`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (engine, name, body))]`. admin.configure
   sugar: validates name (non-empty) + body; builds `PreparedWrite::AdminSchema { name,
   kind: "latest_state", schema_json: body, retention_json: "{}" }`; → `inner.write(&batch)`
   → `PyWriteReceipt`.
2. **`read_get`** — `(engine, logical_id)` → `Option<PyNodeRecord>` via
   `inner.read_get(&logical_id)` (not-found = `None`, never an exception; typed NotFound is
   reserved-gap Slice 31). G2/G3 Slice 30.
3. **`read_get_many`** — `(engine, logical_ids: Bound<PyList>)` →
   `Vec<Option<PyNodeRecord>>` via `inner.read_get_many(&ids)`.
4. **`read_collection`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (engine, collection, after_id=None,
   limit=0))]` → `Vec<PyOpStoreRow>` via `read_collection_impl` →
   `inner.read_collection(&collection, after_id, limit)`. Mandatory limit + after_id cursor.
5. **`read_mutations`** — same signature; also delegates to `read_collection_impl`.
6. **`read_list`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (engine, kind, predicates=None, limit=100))]`
   (G4 Slice 35). predicates = list of dicts `{ "type": "eq"|"gt"|"gte"|"lt"|"lte",
   "path": str, "value": str|int|bool }`; `py_predicate_to_rust` builds `RustPredicate`
   (bool checked before int since Python bool ⊂ int; path validated in Rust →
   `InvalidFilterError`; unknown type → `PyValueError`); →
   `inner.read_list(&kind, &rust_predicates, limit)` → `Vec<PyNodeRecord>`.
7. **`graph_neighbors`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (engine, logical_id, depth, direction))]`
   (G5 Slice 20). depth must be 1..=3 (else `InvalidArgumentError`); direction via
   `parse_direction` ("outgoing"→Outgoing, "incoming"→Incoming, "both"→Both, else
   `InvalidArgumentError`); → `inner.graph_neighbors(&logical_id, depth, dir)`; reachable
   nodes excluding root within depth hops, hard-capped at 50.
8. **`search_expand`** — `#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]`, signature `(engine, query,
   depth, source_type=None, kind=None, created_after=None, status=None)` (G6 Slice 20).
   depth 0..=3; same filter construction; → `inner.search_expand(&query, filter, depth)` →
   `PySearchExpandResult`.
9. **`rerank`** — `#[pyo3(signature = (query, passages, rerank_depth, alpha=None,
   pool_n=None))]` (0.8.2 Slice E2; standalone, NOT engine-bound). Marshals
   `[{"id": int, "body": str, "score": float}]` → `(u64, String, f64)` tuples
   (`dict_u64_required` for id, `dict_str_required` for body, `dict_f64_required` for
   score); defaults `alpha=0.3`, `pool_n=rerank_depth`; runs
   `rust_rerank_passages(&query, tuples, rerank_depth, alpha, pool_n)` inside
   `py.detach(catch_unwind(AssertUnwindSafe(...)))`; outer panic → `PanicException("rerank
   panic (see logs)")`; inner Err (non-finite score) → `WriteValidationError`. Returns
   `Vec<Py<PyDict>>` of `{"id", "score", "ce_score"}` (ce_score = None outside the reranked
   pool). Identity contract: `rerank_depth == 0` OR empty list returns input order +
   input scores (no model load, no network).
10. **`force_panic_for_test`** — `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]`. AC-067
    force-panic probe; `panic!("force_panic_for_test: AC-067 probe")`.

### Helper fns (translation / extraction)
- `translate_batch(batch) -> Vec<PreparedWrite>` (maps `translate_write_item`).
- `dict_get`, `dict_str`, `dict_str_required`, `dict_u64_required`, `dict_f64_required`.
- `translate_write_item` — dispatches on keys `edge`/`op_store`/`admin_schema`/`node`;

- `translate_node` → `PreparedWrite::Node { kind, body(default "{}"), source_id, logical_id }`.

- `translate_op_store` → `PreparedWrite::OpStore { collection, record_key, schema_id, body }`.
- `translate_admin_schema` → `PreparedWrite::AdminSchema { name, kind, schema_json,
  retention_json(default "{}") }`.
- `py_predicate_to_rust`, `parse_direction`.
- `read_collection_impl`.

## 10. Module registration — `#[pymodule(gil_used = true)]`

**`gil_used = true`** — preserves current GIL semantics. pyo3 0.28+ makes `#[pymodule]`
free-threaded by default, but this binding is `abi3-py310` and the whole FFI contract
assumes the GIL is held. Opting into free-threading (`gil_used = false`) is a separate,
larger correctness campaign (see
dev/design/free-threaded-python-value-lift-and-experiments.md).

`fn _fathomdb(py, m: Bound<PyModule>) -> PyResult<()>`:
- `add_class`: PyEngine, PyWriteReceipt, PyIngestWithExtractorReceipt, PySoftFallback,
  PySearchHit, PySearchResult, PyCounterSnapshot, PyMigrationStepReport, PyEmbedderIdentity,
  PyOpenReport, PyNodeRecord, PyOpStoreRow, PyExpandedNode, PySearchExpandResult.
- `add_function`: admin_configure, read_get, read_get_many, read_collection,
  read_mutations, read_list, graph_neighbors, search_expand, rerank; plus
  `force_panic_for_test` under `#[cfg(any(test, feature = "test-hooks"))]`.
- `m.add(...)` for all 22 exception types (see §3).

- `validate_ffi_string_accepts_plain_ascii`
- `validate_ffi_string_accepts_non_ascii_utf8` ("héllo 🦀 文字")
- `validate_ffi_string_rejects_embedded_nul` (asserts diagnostic contains "NUL")
- `validate_ffi_string_rejects_lone_surrogate` (note: U+D800 can't appear in a Rust &str;
